He now eats golden. And at that moment in time, the last thing Herbie's interested in doing is buying a timeshare. Feet are sticking out of the bear's mouth. He's very disturbed. And so this... And the bears know that nothing can be done to him. So the park people come, get him, take him away, put tags on their feet. They're all back the next day. So it was a big problem. I mean, we generate leads all day long, but closing the sales was a nightmare. I mean, you're driving down the road and there's bears in the middle of the road. You got to stop and beep the horn. And it was very disturbing to everybody, right? And so we switched this to not taking people there. That's what we switched this to. So the last six months when I was working with him is in Manhattan, we built a cabin with little trees and a little babbling brook behind it. Everything but the bears, right? Inside a great big warehouse, right? And we had a nice closing room and videos to show. And so we generated leads and we brought everybody down to the warehouse and we sold them in the warehouse and we let them find out about the bears later, right? So there was no way to control the bears. And you can't blame the bears and you really can't blame Golda for feeding it the bagel. And so you can't blame really people around you for not knowing how to behave properly with a seven-figure earner because they ain't never seen a seven-figure earner. They really don't much care either about behaving properly, but that you can fix. But them knowing how, you really can't blame them. So most of the difficulty you have with people's... what we view as their competence is another matter altogether. But I'm really not talking about incompetence at this point. I'm talking about what we would view as uncooperative behavior or interference with our productivity in one way, shape, or form. See, that behavior, when that's happening around you, that's all your fault. It's not theirs because they don't know how to behave around a bear. And if you're not going to train them, now if you train them and they refuse to get it, then that's another issue altogether. You've got to get rid of them, get them away from you. Or severely box off the amount of access and time that you give them. The first thing you got to do is train them. Because most of what we're mad at about these uncooperative, time-sucking, getting in our way, productivity interfering people is like being mad at the salesperson who doesn't close a sale, but we threw them out on the sales floor with no training, no script, no preparation, no role-play. Well, whose fault is that? It's ours, right? So if you have a client, if you have clients who like bother you all the time and think they should be able to call you six times a day, well, whose fault is that? That's, first of all, they've been trained to do that by everybody else they deal with. You haven't trained them to do otherwise with you. So control of others is huge. I mean, it's huge. And you have to consciously and deliberately and somewhat patiently, if it's important to have this person somehow connected with you, you've got to train them on how it is that they are supposed to behave in their relationship with you. And then if they prove untrainable, then you've got to get rid of them and get another one, right? And pretty much you can do that with any category of human. There's more of them than whatever quantity of them you need. So employee, vendor, accountant, lawyer, money manager, kids, you can make more. You can send that one away, go make another one, you know? There's lots of orphans looking for homes, you know? So every one of these categories, if they prove untrainable, ship them out and get them another one, right? But first you've got to train them. But you just have to make up your mind that you are going to control all of the other people around you, see, because you can't afford any slop. Not if you're going to talk about seven figures. If you want to talk about low six figures, you can afford that client who sucks up an inordinate amount of your time but has been with you forever and you go fishing once a year with them and so you don't want to be rude to them. And you can afford that.
